summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and secrets to hardcoded C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that queries the AWS EC2 instance metadata service (IMDSv2) for the instance role's temporary credentials, then enumerates all regions calling `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value` to dump every secret. It additionally reads /root/.npmrc and scrapes environment variables matching HTB|FLAG|SECRET. The harvested data is chunked and PUT over plain HTTP to http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-*. Execution is gated by a hostname regex matching 12-hex Docker container IDs and the presence of /home/node or /app, so the payload stays dormant on developer laptops and only fires in containerized CI/cloud environments. The destinations are hardcoded in postinstall.js (TARGETS = ["http://127.0.0.1:30782", "http://154.57.164.71:30782"]).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
